description_long: This object is a vintage film projector, featuring a rectangular body with a combination of black and light gray surfaces. Two large, circular film reels are mounted on the top, one at the front and one at the rear, connected by a visible film path. The front panel displays a set of control knobs, switches, and indicator lights, along with a prominent brand label. The lens assembly protrudes from the front, surrounded by adjustment rings and mechanical components. The sides of the projector are relatively plain, with some small ports and screws, while the back is mostly flat with a few labels and a power input. The overall design is robust, with metallic and plastic materials, and the object sits on small rubber feet for stability.
description: A vintage film projector with dual large reels, a black and gray body, and a detailed front control panel with knobs and switches.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile, film reels, and the protruding lens assembly.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the flat black back panel, rear film reel, and minimal controls.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with the film reels and lens visible from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the control panel, brand label, lens, and both film reels prominently.
attribute_brand_logo: The logo 'BAUER' is visible on the front left side of the projector, in yellow and black, with a rectangular background and clear, bold lettering.
attribute_lens_type: fixed lens with adjustment rings, medium size, protruding from the front panel
attribute_control_panel_design: The control panel is located on the front lower section, featuring three round knobs, a few switches, and indicator lights, all clearly labeled for user interaction.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: Ventilation slats are visible on the right side of the projector body, arranged in a horizontal pattern for airflow.
